folio.label: 89v
folio.l: Go lede hi~ to the detú , I charge the

note.notetxt: 318 The colour of the ink in this folio becomes lighter brown. I suspect it started on 89r, but it is very clear only in the verso. This same colour will carry on until the end of the tale. Double check.ODR

folio.l: And happed as they wenten , by the weye
Toward the place , there he shuld deye
The knygút cam , whicú æ men wenden , had ben ded
Than thougúten they , it were the best red
To lede hem bothe , to the Iuge a geyne
They seiden lord , the knygút ne hatú nat slayne
His felawe , heere he stant hool a lyue
¢ ñe shuln be deede q¶ he , so mote I thryue
This is to seyn , bothe on two and three
And to the first knygút , rigút thus spak he
I dampned the , thow must algate be deed
And thow also , must nedesnedes lese thyn hed
For thow art cause , why thy felawe deyetú
And to the thridde knygút , rigút thus he seitú
Thow hast nat do , that I comaunded the
And thus he dide , do slee hem alle three
¢ Irrous Cambises , was thus eke dronkelewe
And ay delited hi~ , to ben a shrewe
And so byfel , a lord of his meyne
That loued vertuous , moralite
Seide on a day , betwix he~ two rigút thus
A lord is lost , if he be vicious
And dronkenesse eke , is a foul recorde
Of any man , and namely in a lorde
There is ful many an eye , & many an ere
A waytynge on a man , he wot nat where
For goddis loue , drynketú more attemprely
Wyn maketú a man , to leese wrecchedly
Hys mynde , & eke hise lymes euäychone
¢ The reuers shalt thow se , q¶ he a none
And preeue it , be thyn owen ex¹ience
That wyn ne dotú to folk nono swicú offence
There is no wyn , byreuetú me my mygút
Of hond ne foot , ne of myn eyen sigút
And for despyt , he drank ful mechel more
An hundred part , than he had don byfore
And rigút anon , this Irrous cursed wrecche
This knygútis sone , let byfore hi~ fecche
Comaundynge hi~ , he shulde byfore hi~ stonde
And sodeynly , he tok his bowe in honde
And vp the stryng , he pulletú to his Ere
And witú an arwe , he slow the child rigút there
Now wheither haue I , a syker hand or noone
Quod he , is al my mygút , and mynde a gone
Hatú wyn byreued me , myn eyen sigút
